    <item>
      <title>Thunder's Shai Gilgeous-Alexander wins appropriate NBA award in landslide</title>
      <description>After winning 2025-2026 Clutch Player of the Year, Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander seemed ice-cold, just like whenever he knocks down a game-winning pull-up jumper. </description>

    <item>
      <title>Victor Wembanyama's scary fall puts Spurs' playoff chances in peril</title>
      <description>After a scary fall put San Antonio Spurs center Victor Wembanyama in the concussion protocol early in Game 2, his team will have to scramble to win without the Defensive Player of the Year.</description>

        <media:title> San Antonio Spurs forward Victor Wembanyama falls to the ground during the first half of Game 2 of the first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs at Frost Bank Center. </media:title>

      <title>VJ Edgecombe's historic performance helps 76ers respond from Game 1 dud in win over Celtics</title>
      <description>After looking like a team that stood little chance against the Boston Celtics in a lackluster Game 1 performance on Sunday, the Philadelphia 76ers evened the series at one game apiece on Tuesday. </description>

    <item>
      <title>Jimmy Butler may not return in 2026-27</title>
      <description>The Golden State Warriors signed Jimmy Butler to an extension last season in hopes of getting two seasons with him alongside Steph Curry. They may end up only getting half a season. </description>

      <title>Hawks' CJ McCollum becomes newest MSG villain in Game 2 win over Knicks</title>
      <description>CJ McCollum took over for Trae Young as the Atlanta Hawks' starting point guard this season. In Monday's Game 2, he took over for Young as Madison Square Garden's No. 1 villain.</description>
      <pubDate>20 Apr 2026 23:20:21 -0400</pubDate>
      <link>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/hawks_cj_mccollum_becomes_newest_msg_villain_in_game_2_win_over_knicks/s1_13132_43751941</link>

      <author>Sean Keane, Yardbarker</author>

        <media:credit> Brad Penner-Imagn Images </media:credit>
        <media:title> Atlanta Hawks guard CJ McCollum (3) brings the ball up court against the New York Knicks during the fourth quarter of Game 2 of the first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs at Madison Square Garden. </media:title>
